meaning: adj.权威的,有权威的,命令的
examples: He has a commanding presence and deep, authoritative voice.
The first authoritative study of polio was published in 1840.
Amanda launched into an authoritative disquisition about contracts.
Derived from the Greek word for "circle, " an encyclical is among the church's most authoritative teaching documents.
Look at the earnings forecasts from Thomson First Call, the authoritative database of analyst reports.
